name: vscode-agent-platform-connector
author: Blinkk
description: Adds Gemini and Claude on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (formerly Vertex AI) to VS Code chat as a native language model provider, using your own gcloud credentials.

scripts.release: pnpm run package && vsce publish --azure-credential --no-dependencies --packagePath vscode-agent-platform-connector.vsix
scripts.release:ovsx: pnpm run package && ovsx publish vscode-agent-platform-connector.vsix --no-dependencies

blinkkAgentPlatformConnector.project.markdownDescription:
**Required.** GCP project that Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (formerly Vertex AI) usage is billed to. Sent as both the request URL `projects/<project>/...` and the `x-goog-user-project` header.

**Prerequisites for this project:**
1. The [`gcloud` CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) is installed and you have signed in (use the **Sign In** command).
2. The Vertex AI API is enabled and your account has the **Vertex AI User** role.
3. Each model you want to use is enabled in this project's [Model Garden](https://console.cloud.google.com/agent-platform/model-garden) (Gemini is usually enabled by default; Claude models must be enabled and their terms accepted).

If empty, the connector falls back to the `GOOGLE_AGENT_PLATFORM_PROJECT` environment variable or the `project` key in the config file. The `GOOGLE_AGENT_PLATFORM_PROJECT` env var, if set, overrides this setting.

markdownEnumDescriptions: Use your global Application Default Credentials (`gcloud auth application-default login`).
Use the connector's own private gcloud credential store; populate it once via the **Sign In (isolated credentials)** command.
blinkkAgentPlatformConnector.authMode.markdownDescription: How the connector obtains a Google access token. Requires the [`gcloud` CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) on your PATH. Overridden by the `GOOGLE_AGENT_PLATFORM_AUTH_MODE` env var if set.

blinkkAgentPlatformConnector.authAccount.markdownDescription: Optional gcloud account to pin (`gcloud ... --account=`). Usually unnecessary. Overridden by the `GOOGLE_AGENT_PLATFORM_AUTH_ACCOUNT` env var if set.

blinkkAgentPlatformConnector.debug.markdownDescription: Enable verbose logging in the **Blinkk Agent Platform Chat Connector** output channel.

blinkkAgentPlatformConnector.geminiLocat.markdownDescription: Vertex location for Gemini models (e.g. `global`, `us-central1`). Leave empty for the default (`global`). Overridden by the `GOOGLE_AGENT_PLATFORM_GEMINI_LOCATION` env var if set.

blinkkAgentPlatformConnector.claudeLocat.markdownDescription: Vertex location for Claude models (e.g. `global`, `us-east5`). Leave empty for the default (`global`). Overridden by the `GOOGLE_AGENT_PLATFORM_CLAUDE_LOCATION` env var if set.

blinkkAgentPlatformConnector.customModel.markdownDescription:
Additional models to expose, **beyond** the built-in defaults (Gemini and Claude). Use this to run any model your project has enabled in [Model Garden](https://console.cloud.google.com/agent-platform/model-garden) without waiting for a code change. The built-in defaults always stay available; a custom entry whose `id` matches a built-in id overrides that built-in.

Each entry is an object. `id` and `name` are required; `api` is inferred from the id when omitted (`messages` for Claude/Anthropic, otherwise `chat`).

Example:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "google/gemini-3-pro",
    "name": "Gemini 3 Pro",
    "api": "chat",
    "maxInputTokens": 1048576,
    "maxOutputTokens": 65535,
    "vision": true
  }
]
```

id.markdownDescription: Picker/upstream model id, e.g. `google/gemini-3-pro` or `claude-opus-4-8`. Append `#thinking` to expose an extended-reasoning variant.

name.markdownDescription: Human-readable label shown in the model picker (the project is appended automatically).

markdownEnumDescriptions: OpenAI Chat Completions shape (Gemini / openapi endpoint).
api.markdownDescription: Upstream API shape. Inferred from the id when omitted.

upstream.markdownDescription: Optional upstream Vertex model id, if it differs from `id` (e.g. for a `#thinking` variant).

vision.markdownDescription: Whether the model accepts image input.
